fields.label: Congregation
fields.value: All Saints of the Desert Church

fields.label: Details
fields.value:
The Total Compensation (stipend plus housing allowance) is $91,000. The Compensation will take into consideration the skills and experience of the priest.  

There are two services each weekend. One service on Saturday and one service on Sunday.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: There is no rectory on the site.

fields.label: Continuing Education Details
fields.value:
2 weeks (standard)

There is a Continuing Education budget between $1,500-$2,000.

fields.value:
Grace Episcopal Church seeks a part-time priest who is warm, caring and enjoys a life of service.  A priest who would like to help others develop their spiritual gifts and offers thought-provoking sermons. A priest who will lead with a sense of fun while being a creative problem solver.  
The leadership of Grace is open to receiving a priest that would like a flexible work schedule.
Kindly submit your materials, cover letter, resume and Vocation Hub profile or OTM pdf during this transition time to the Rev. Canon Anita Braden.

fields.label: Applicant narrative questions
fields.value: 1. How do you approach pastoral care in your ministry?, 3. What does stewardship mean to you, and how do you practice it?, 12. Describe a recent ministry experiment or innovation.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: The Vicar's position is part-time and the Total Compensation offered is forty-three thousand dollars.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: There is no rectory provided on the site.

fields.label: Details
fields.value:
Given the position is part-time and less than1500 hours per year, no medical is available for the position.  

Pension benefits are available for this position.

fields.label: Vacation & Leave Details
fields.value: Standard vacation time, Continuing Education of two weeks, seven days of spiritual retreat time.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value: Please review the parish profile that is located on the congregation's website.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
Our search committee held multiple listening sessions with our parishioners. We received valuable input that we synthesized into four attributes we desire in our candidates for Associate
Rector:

Strategic Catalyst for Dynamic Ministries
* Discovers parishioners’ talents and gifts and inspires them to use their gifts to grow St. Paul’s and its
impact on each other and the community.
* Is willing to experiment and learn by developing intergenerational and other creative programming.

Engaging Relationship Developer
* Passionately builds relationships with children, youth, and families, cultivating connection with our
diocese, the wider community, and the life of the church through shared ministries.
* Values diversity and adapts to different settings and people with pastoral sensitivity.
* Brings a healthy sense of humor to ministry.

Effective Collaborative Leader
* Seeks collaboration with parishioners and with other clergy and staff, fostering a culture of belonging.
* Is intrinsically motivated and organized. Values accountability and quality communication.

Inspirational Spiritual Guide
* Helps create and oversee compelling children and youth formation by balancing biblical knowledge and spiritual growth programming with engaging activities at church and in the community.
* Impacts parishioners through sermons, reflections, spiritual practices, discussions and activities.

fields.label: Leadership skills (maximum of 5)
fields.value: Appreciative Inquiry, Collaborative Leadership, Congregational Development, Strategic Planning, Quality Communication

fields.label: Ministry skills  (maximum of 10)
fields.value: Formation, Intergenerational Formation, Pastoral Care, Prayer & Spirituality, Laity Empowerment

fields.value: We seek a rector who is a natural relationship builder, interested in the lives of their congregants, who can enhance the loving spirit that identifies our community.

fields.label: Applicant narrative questions
fields.value: 1. How do you approach pastoral care in your ministry?, 2. How are you preparing yourself for the Church of the future?, 3. What does stewardship mean to you, and how do you practice it?, 4. How have you experienced and addressed conflict in ministry?, 5. What has been your experience leading change in the Church?, 6. How do you incorporate accessibility and inclusion in your ministry?, 8. How do you use technology in your ministry?, 9. In what ways have you grown in cultural competency as a leader?, 11. How has your theology evolved over time?, 13. What is a ministry risk you've taken, and what came of it?, 14. How do you discern and adapt to the changing needs of your community?, 15. How do you take care of your well-being-spiritually, emotionally, and physically?, 16. In what ways are you engaged in your wider Church or local community?

fields.label: This is a position serving multiple congregations or organizations.

fields.value: Located on opposite sides of the CT river, these two congregations have laid the groundwork for together welcoming a priest for a FT call. St. James is located in downtown Glastonbury with a nice physical plant and well-positioned to engaged in ministry to the community. Trinity is located in Old Wethersfield in a lovely historic building. They are excited for what the future could hold for them.
Apply on https://www.episcopalct.org/open-positions/clergy-employment/ and look for St. James Glastonbury and Trinity Wethersfield.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: $138k to 162k depending on years of experience.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: 30k housing stipend (figured into above compensation calculation).
